rationale: Dominant medical-robotics platform with widening installed base and procedure-volume tailwind from elective-surgery recovery. Profile names robotics as a next-wave bucket; ISRG diversifies BOTZ exposure into healthcare robotics.

First publicly traded pure-play quantum-computing company; profile explicitly names quantum as a next-wave priority and prior to IONQ's Oct 1, 2021 listing there was no liquid US-listed pure-play. Trapped-ion approach is differentiated from superconducting peers and accessible via AWS Braket and Azure Quantum.

Sole North American manufacturer of large naval nuclear reactors plus growing Canadian SMR supply-chain footprint via partnerships with NuScale and GE Hitachi. Hits both nuclear (next-wave) and defense exposure; complements URA/CCJ (uranium fuel cycle) with downstream reactor manufacturing.

Data-center CPU and accelerator share gainer; pending Xilinx acquisition (announced Oct 2020, regulatory approvals progressing through 2021) brings FPGA and AI-inference IP that broadens AMD's AI silicon stack. Diversifies AI-compute exposure beyond NVDA/TSM concentration.

The watchlist currently has zero direct demographics-aging exposure, yet the profile names demographics as a non-tech next wave. XLV (Health Care Select Sector SPDR, launched 1998, deeply liquid) is the cleanest US-listed broad healthcare vehicle: large-cap pharma (JNJ, PFE, LLY, MRK), insurers (UNH), and devicemakers, all of which earn revenue from aging US/EU/Japan populations. Also provides a defensive ballast against the tech-heavy AI sleeve during the ongoing rate-hike repricing.

Pure-play US merchant nuclear utility (largest carbon-free generator in the country) that spun off from Exelon Feb 1, 2022 and now trades cleanly as a single name; complements the URA/CCJ mining exposure and BWXT reactor-services exposure already in the watchlist with the actual operator of the existing US reactor fleet, which is the direct beneficiary of the April 2022 federal civil-nuclear-credit program.

Prime contractor for the US Space Development Agency's low-Earth-orbit constellation and the Space Force's deep-space radar, providing single-name exposure to the militarized-space buildout that the user's wave thesis names (rockets/spacecraft) and that complements RKLB (small launch) and ARKX (broad space ETF) with a profitable mega-cap operator; the ITA defense ETF in the current watchlist underweights space specifically.

Machine-vision and barcode-reader maker that supplies the 'eyes' for factory and warehouse automation; provides a profitable, listed-since-1989 single-name robotics holding to pair with the BOTZ industrial-robotics ETF and ISRG surgical-robotics, addressing the user's named 'robotics' next-wave bucket with a company whose Q1 2022 results already show the wave is in commercial buildup (logistics now its largest end market, +18% YoY revenue).

Eli Lilly's Mounjaro (tirzepatide) is the cleanest pure-play on the aging-population/obesity demographics wave; Q1 2023 sales of ~$570M plus the SURMOUNT-2 readout position it for an obesity-label FDA decision later this year, and the broad XLV ETF is the only demographics exposure in the current watchlist.

NuScale Power is the only publicly-traded pure-play SMR developer; its 50 MWe design received final NRC certification in January 2023 and the company has since announced an MOU with Nucor for steel-mill baseload and a $275M public-private commitment for a Romania deployment, giving the watchlist a fission-SMR pure-play that URA/CCJ/BWXT/CEG do not provide.

Cognex's Q1 2023 results (May 4) flagged a step-down in factory-automation demand with e-commerce logistics customers pausing capex and consumer-electronics/semicap customers weakening — the fundamental robotics thesis is breaking, and BOTZ + ISRG continue to give the watchlist diversified robotics exposure.

Vertiv is the cleanest AI-data-center picks-and-shovels play: its Q2 2023 print (Aug 2) showed 24% revenue growth, 25% organic, and a $200M raise to FY23 FCF guidance, with management explicitly attributing the acceleration to AI-driven hyperscaler infrastructure spend — adds industrial-side AI capex exposure that the semiconductor-heavy AI sub-bucket (NVDA/AMD/TSM) does not capture.

Broadcom is the second-largest beneficiary of AI buildout after NVDA via custom ASICs (Google TPU) and AI networking silicon, and the VMware acquisition closed in November 2023, adding recurring software revenue.

Global X Uranium ETF gives diversified uranium miners exposure complementing CCJ; spot uranium broke above $80/lb in late 2023 (highest since 2008) on supply deficits, COP28 nuclear pledges, and restart momentum.
Broad healthcare ETF lagged S&P meaningfully in 2023 (+2% vs +24%) and the demographics/GLP-1 theme is already captured more directly via LLY; freeing the slot for higher-conviction adds.

Symbotic is the cleanest US-listed pure-play on warehouse robotics with a Walmart anchor partnership and an accelerating revenue ramp ratified by its Feb-2024 quarter; a more thesis-aligned robotics expression than the BOTZ ETF it replaces.

Vistra owns the Comanche Peak nuclear plant plus a large merchant gas/coal fleet sitting next to Texas data-center demand growth; the September 2023 closing of the Energy Harbor acquisition added Davis-Besse/Perry/Beaver Valley nuclear, making VST the largest competitive nuclear operator after Constellation and a direct beneficiary of the AI-power thesis the profile names.

NuScale's flagship UAMPS Carbon Free Power Project was canceled in November 2023 due to cost overruns, a fundamental thesis breach for a pre-revenue SPAC whose only near-term commercial validation was that project; SMR exposure is better held through CCJ/CEG/BWXT/URA which have real cash flows or diversified miner exposure.

ASML is the sole supplier of EUV lithography for leading-edge logic and HBM DRAM, the chokepoint in AI-chip manufacturing; its Q1-2024 print showed a Q/Q book-to-bill inflection that confirms a 2025 cyclical upturn driven by AI capex, filling the open 24th watchlist slot with the cleanest semicap expression of the AI infrastructure leg.

GE Vernova is the cleanest pure-play on the AI-power buildout: gas turbines, grid equipment, and a nuclear services arm (BWRX-300 SMR design with Hitachi) all sitting in front of a structural utility-capex up-cycle that the Q3-2024 Constellation/Microsoft Three Mile Island PPA crystallized; spun out of GE in April 2024 and now has two quarters of standalone prints confirming order growth and margin inflection.

The Global X Uranium ETF is now the weakest expression of the nuclear thesis on a watchlist that already holds CCJ (the dominant Western miner and URA's top holding at ~22%), CEG and VST (operating nuclear fleets directly levered to the AI-power PPA cycle), and BWXT (nuclear services and naval reactors); adding GEV in the same call further saturates nuclear-services exposure, so URA is the cleanest redundant remove to free a slot under the 24-ticker cap.

Google's Willow chip announcement on Dec 9, 2024 is a sector-level inflection for quantum computing; adding D-Wave (annealing architecture) diversifies the quantum bucket alongside trapped-ion incumbent IONQ.
Palantir's Q3 2024 U.S. revenue grew 44% YoY on AIP demand and the company landed a 750M GBP UK defense agreement, fitting both the AI wave and the geopolitical/defense thesis with one ticker.
Symbotic disclosed it cannot file its 10-K on time (Nov 27, 2024), an accounting/governance flag that breaches the original clean-execution thesis; ISRG remains the robotics anchor.

European Commission President von der Leyen unveiled an 800B euro ReArm Europe plan on March 4, 2025 after Trump paused Ukraine aid, marking a structural geopolitical wave inflection; EUAD is the cleanest US-listed pure-play European aerospace and defense ETF (launched Oct 22, 2024) and broadens the watchlist's geopolitical-defense exposure beyond US primes ITA and NOC.

DeepSeek R1 (Jan 27, 2025) crystallized a 'cheap inference' narrative that hits second-tier AI-accelerator names hardest, and AMD's MI300 ramp has been visibly disappointing relative to NVDA share gains and Street expectations; the watchlist already has NVDA, AVGO, TSM, and ASML covering AI-semis, and the AI bucket is overstacked at 7 tickers versus the 4-per-bucket curator guideline.

The robotics bucket on the current watchlist is a single ticker (ISRG, surgical-only) while the user's thesis explicitly names robotics as a next wave; BOTZ is the most liquid US-listed pure-play robotics-and-automation ETF and broadens exposure to industrial-automation leaders (Fanuc, Keyence, ABB) and humanoid-adjacent enablers alongside its NVDA/ISRG overlap.

ASML is the most exposed name in the AI-semis bucket (8 tickers, over the 4-per-bucket guideline) to the US chip-export-control overhang on China, which was ~33% of 2024 system sales; the AI-equipment thesis is already well-covered by NVDA, AVGO, and TSM downstream, and freeing a slot lets the watchlist add a robotics ETF the user's thesis calls for.

Broadens the thin rockets/spacecraft bucket (currently only RKLB) into the satellite-constellation layer with the only US-listed pure-play on direct-to-cell-handset satellite broadband, anchored by signed commercial contracts with AT&T and Verizon and multiple BlueBird operational satellites on orbit through 2025.

D-Wave has rerated more than 10x off late-2024 lows on quantum-hype enthusiasm with revenue still in the low tens of millions and the commercial annealing thesis narrower than gate-model peers; trimming to one quantum name (IONQ, which has the stronger trapped-ion commercial pipeline, AWS Braket integration, and multiple government contracts) keeps the bucket exposed to the thesis while cutting the most peak-enthusiasm-stretched holding.
